part.text:
# Split a Commit in Jujutsu

## Background
You are working on a project using `jj` (Jujutsu). You have a single commit that introduces two features at once, but you realize they should be separated into two distinct commits for a cleaner history.

## Requirements
- The repository is located at `/home/user/myproject`.
- The parent of the current working copy (`@-`) contains two files: `feature_a.py` and `feature_b.py`, and has the description "Add feature A and feature B".
- You must split this commit into two separate commits.
- The first commit (older) must contain ONLY `feature_a.py` and have the description exactly "Add feature A".
- The second commit (newer, child of the first) must contain ONLY `feature_b.py` and have the description exactly "Add feature B".
- The current working copy (`@`) should remain empty and be a child of the "Add feature B" commit.

## Constraints
- Project path: `/home/user/myproject`
- Use only `jj` commands.

# Split a Commit in Jujutsu

## Background
You are working on a project using `jj` (Jujutsu). You have a single commit that introduces two features at once, but you realize they should be separated into two distinct commits for a cleaner history.

## Requirements
- The repository is located at `/home/user/myproject`.
- The parent of the current working copy (`@-`) contains two files: `feature_a.py` and `feature_b.py`, and has the description "Add feature A and feature B".
- You must split this commit into two separate commits.
- The first commit (older) must contain ONLY `feature_a.py` and have the description exactly "Add feature A".
- The second commit (newer, child of the first) must contain ONLY `feature_b.py` and have the description exactly "Add feature B".
- The current working copy (`@`) should remain empty and be a child of the "Add feature B" commit.

## Constraints
- Project path: `/home/user/myproject`
- Use only `jj` commands.

input.command: cd /home/user/myproject && jj split -r @- -m "Add feature A" feature_a.py
cd /home/user/myproject && jj split -r @- -m "Add feature A" feature_a.py
cd /home/user/myproject && jj split -r @- -m "Add feature A" feature_a.py

Rebased 1 descendant commits
Selected changes : wkorwkxr 5250f4b3 Add feature A
Remaining changes: tzouyqlp 69dcf94c Add feature A and feature B
Working copy  (@) now at: vkoomxul 1dfe9a4f (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : tzouyqlp 69dcf94c Add feature A and feature B

Rebased 1 descendant commits
Working copy  (@) now at: vkoomxul 9c48bb47 (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : tzouyqlp 0447bf7b Add feature B

input.command: cd /home/user/myproject && jj log -r '::@' && echo "---" && jj diff -r @- && echo "---" && jj
cd /home/user/myproject && jj log -r '::@' && echo "---" && jj diff -r @- && echo "---" && jj diff -r @--
cd /home/user/myproject && jj log -r '::@' && echo "---" && jj diff -r @- && echo "---" && jj diff -r @--
cd /home/user/myproject && jj log -r '::@' && echo "---" && jj diff -r @- && echo "---" && jj diff -r @--

output.output:
@  vkoomxul test@example.com 2026-04-22 05:02:18 9c48bb47
│  (empty) (no description set)
○  tzouyqlp test@example.com 2026-04-22 05:02:17 0447bf7b
│  Add feature B
○  wkorwkxr test@example.com 2026-04-22 05:02:11 5250f4b3
│  Add feature A
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000
---
Added regular file feature_b.py:
        1: print('B')
---
Added regular file feature_a.py:
        1: print('A')
